title: Fear Not Those Who Kill the Body
subtitle: Christ arms his friends against the fear of death and bids them confess him before men

stillness: Be still, and let the noise of the world fall away.
There is great security in fearing nothing but God.
Cast out the fear of the world, and rest.
gospel: I say to you, my friends:
Fear not those who kill the body,
and after that have no more that they can do.
They slay a man in the body only, and for a time,
but the soul they cannot kill.
who is able to destroy both body and soul in hell.
behold: After the fear of persecution and of curses,
the Lord sets before his disciples the fear of bodily death,
which is the last of all things terrible in this world, and the end.
Yet he does not promise them rescue from death;
he promises something greater than if he had not let them suffer.
For to persuade a man to despise death
is a greater thing than to snatch him from it.
See how the hand of the Almighty no one can escape,
neither the living nor the dead.
And the body, even if it be not slain by the wicked for God,
after a little while dies of itself, by nature.
So those slain in the body by the wicked lose nothing
but the delaying of their death.
consider: Not only is he a betrayer of truth who openly speaks a lie for the truth,
but also he who does not freely proclaim the truth that ought to be proclaimed,
and he who keeps silent through fear of those who can kill the body.
And what do I say? That through fear of death men keep silent about the truth!
For the wretched belly, and for the hope of empty honor, men keep silent about the truth.
If a captain of the Roman Emperor saw the city stormed by enemies and, able to free it, neglected to defend it,
would he not seem to have betrayed what he could have saved?
So you, seeing the truth of God assailed by the wicked, able to defend it, and keeping silent, have assailed it yourself.
What is this folly of ours: to hate what is ours, and to love what is not ours?
To labor for what we cannot keep, and to neglect what we shall have forever?
What is the fear of death? Not the pain of leaving the body, but despair of life after death.
He fears death who does not believe he will live after death.
He who has an evil conscience is always faint-hearted and timid.
Let there be in you a singular and surpassing trust in Christ, in his promises and his words, in every peril and doubt;
so that, though all the camps of the demons and the whole synagogue of sinners besiege you, you count them all as flying smoke, as tow, as a thin and empty froth.
Every working of the enemy is like this: at first his assault is heavy and almost unbearable; but if a man bears it with a brave spirit, he finds it weaker the second time,
and the more it is beaten back, the more it grows cold.
prayer: grant me so to despise all earthly desires and sinful delights
that I may fear to suffer nothing on earth for your name;
but let your name and your Passion be so inscribed forever upon my heart
that my soul may deserve to hold the palm of victory against all things adverse and prosperous,
and so confess you in heart, in word, and in deed before men on earth,
that you, according to your promise, may confess me before your Father in heaven,
where by your tenderness and mercy, with your saints and your elect,
I may deserve to receive the joys promised through you, good Jesus, to those who thus confess you. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Magna securitas est nihil timere praeter Deum, et stultus timor est, quo plus timetur humana indignatio, quam divina.
latinJewel.english: It is a great security to fear nothing but God; and foolish is the fear by which human anger is dreaded more than the divine.
